events.description: 6-hour website development competition

participation.eligibility: All students from any department or year

rounds.name: Development & Presentation
rounds.subtitle: Build, pitch, and defend your web solution
rounds.description: Participants build a complete website under time constraints and present the concept, impact, and technical implementation to judges.

details.primary_overview: Webathon is a 6-hour fast-paced competition where participants design and develop innovative websites based on a given theme or problem statement. It is a platform to showcase technical skills, creativity, and problem-solving under time constraints while presenting real-world impact.
details.what_is: The Webathon gives aspiring web developers a stage to design and build innovative website solutions. Participants work in a time-bound environment and present their ideas and implementations before judges.
who_can_participate: Open to all students from any department and year
Individual or team participation allowed
Team size: 1 to 2 members
event_timeline: Registration Fee: INR 100 per participant (team fee INR 200)

rules_and_regulations.title: Participation Guidelines
points: Participants can compete individually or in teams
All team members must be registered before the event begins
Participants must carry their own laptops and required accessories

rules_and_regulations.title: Development Rules
points: Competition duration is strictly 6 hours
Problem statement or theme will be shared at the start
The website must be developed during event hours only
Open-source libraries, frameworks, and APIs are allowed
Pre-built templates or previously developed projects are not allowed
Internet usage is permitted for development only; plagiarism is prohibited

rules_and_regulations.title: Submission Guidelines
points: Final project must be submitted before the deadline
Submission must include source code and a working demo
Late submissions are not accepted

rules_and_regulations.title: Presentation and Demonstration
points: 5 minutes for idea presentation and relevance
3 to 4 minutes for live website demonstration
2 to 3 minutes for Q&A with judges

rules_and_regulations.title: Judging Criteria
points: Innovation and creativity
UI/UX design and responsiveness
Functionality and technical implementation
Relevance to problem statement
Presentation clarity

rules_and_regulations.title: General Rules
points: Any form of plagiarism or unfair means leads to disqualification
Teams must maintain discipline and follow event guidelines
Judges' decisions are final and binding

details.team_structure: The competition is open to individuals or teams consisting of up to two members.
additional_information: Participation includes certificates and refreshments
For queries, contact Sarthi Patil at 8446639066

events.description: Innovation and idea pitching competition

rounds.name: Idea Presentation
rounds.subtitle: Pitch your problem-solution concept
rounds.description: Teams present an innovative app idea, explain the core problem-solution flow, and respond to expert questions.

details.primary_overview: Idea Carnival provides a platform for students to present innovative mobile application solutions while demonstrating technical skills, creativity, and business acumen in front of experts.
details.what_is: Open Innovation Ideathon Carnival allows students to propose practical solutions to real-world problems and showcase structured problem-solving and innovation thinking.
who_can_participate: Open to all students from any department and year
Team participation only
Team size: 2 or 4 members

rules_and_regulations.title: Presentation and Demonstration
points: Each team gets 15 minutes total
8 minutes for presentation of concept and significance
2 minutes for optional prototype demonstration
5 minutes for Q&A with judging panel

rules_and_regulations.title: Evaluation Focus
points: Innovation and originality
Clarity of problem and solution framing
Feasibility and impact
Quality of presentation and communication

details.team_structure: The competition is open to teams of two or four members.
additional_information: Participation includes certificates and refreshments
For queries, contact Suraj Munde at 7741007540

events.description: Treasure hunt with coding and aptitude challenges

rounds.name: Aptitude and Coding Quiz
rounds.subtitle: Round 1
rounds.description: Teams solve aptitude and coding questions to qualify for the hunt phase.

rounds.name: Treasure Hunt
rounds.subtitle: Round 2
rounds.description: Qualified teams solve clue chains and location-based puzzles across campus.

rounds.name: Buzzer Round
rounds.subtitle: Round 3
rounds.description: Final shortlisted teams compete in a rapid buzzer format to decide winners.

details.primary_overview: Infinity Hunt is a treasure hunt-style competition blending aptitude, coding, puzzles, and clue-solving where teams race against time to reach the final destination.
details.what_is: Infinity Hunt combines discovery, mental agility, and technical thinking. Participants crack clues, solve coding and aptitude challenges, and move through progressive rounds.
who_can_participate: Open to all students from any department and year
Team participation only
Team size: exactly 4 members
event_timeline: Registration Fee: INR 100 per participant (team fee INR 400)

rules_and_regulations.title: Mandatory Attendance
points: All participants must report on time and attend the full event duration
Late entry or early exit may lead to disqualification

rules_and_regulations.title: Group Participation Etiquette
points: Group members must stay together during the hunt
Splitting up to solve clues individually is not allowed

rules_and_regulations.title: Venue Conduct
points: Do not damage property or tamper with infrastructure
Any such behavior leads to immediate disqualification

rules_and_regulations.title: No External Help
points: Use of mobile phones, internet, or outsider help is prohibited unless explicitly allowed
All answers must be solved independently by the team

rules_and_regulations.title: Clue Integrity
points: Do not remove, hide, or alter clues
If a clue for another team is found, report it to coordinators

details.team_structure: Infinity Hunt is open to teams of four members.
additional_information: Participation includes certificates and refreshments
For queries, contact Atharva Bhasme at 9322142632

events.description: Placement simulation competition

rounds.name: Aptitude Test
rounds.subtitle: Round 1
rounds.description: Candidates face placement-style screening with quantitative, reasoning, and technical MCQ sections.

rounds.name: Coding Round
rounds.subtitle: Round 2
rounds.description: Shortlisted participants solve coding problems on HackerRank under strict evaluation constraints.

rounds.name: Debugging Round
rounds.subtitle: Round 3
rounds.description: Participants identify and fix errors in provided code to complete the hiring simulation journey.

details.primary_overview: PlaceX is a national-level hiring simulation challenge that replicates company placement processes across aptitude, coding, and debugging rounds.
details.what_is: PlaceX helps students practice a real placement journey under time pressure and assessment conditions similar to corporate hiring pipelines.
who_can_participate: Open to students across India
Individual participation only

rules_and_regulations.title: Competition Conduct
points: Participants must follow all round instructions
Any unfair means results in disqualification
Judges' decisions are final
Round-wise time limits must be strictly followed

details.team_structure: Only individual participation is allowed.
additional_information: All participants receive certificates
For queries, contact Saniya Mane (8799879400) or Viraj Potdar (9764482435)

events.description: Solo and duo reel-making competition capturing the energy of Technotsav across Day 1 and Day 2.
events.venue: DY Patil College of Engineering & Technology (DYPCET), Kolhapur

participation.eligibility: Open to all students of any department or year

rounds.name: Round 1 - One Shot, One Reel
rounds.subtitle: Theme-based production
rounds.description: Participants conceptualize, shoot, and edit a maximum 90-second reel based on Technotsav highlights.
steps: Shoot event highlights from Day 1 and Day 2 within college premises
Edit in portrait format (9:16) with maximum duration of 90 seconds
Submit final reel via Google Drive link within upload window

details.primary_overview: Reel Rumble is a content creation competition where participants document Technotsav's atmosphere, moments, and storytelling through short-form video reels.
details.what_is: Participants create and submit an original reel that captures inauguration, activities, and closing highlights in a compelling narrative format.
who_can_participate: Open to all students of any department or year
Solo participation or group participation up to 2 members
event_timeline: Day 1 Reporting: 8:30 AM | Shooting starts: 9:00 AM
Day 2 Reporting: 8:30 AM | Shooting window: 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM
Submission window opens after closing ceremony and remains active for 2 hours

rules_and_regulations.title: General Rules
points: Students from diploma and undergraduate programs are eligible
Participants must carry valid college ID cards
No spot entries allowed; fees are non-refundable
Judges' decision is final
Misconduct with volunteers, judges, or peers results in disqualification

rules_and_regulations.title: Reel Guidelines
points: Maximum reel duration: 90 seconds
Format: Portrait 9:16 suitable for Instagram
Content must be shot inside DYPCET premises during Technotsav
Participants must cover both inauguration and closing ceremony
Do not post or share reels publicly before official results

rules_and_regulations.title: Submission Rules
points: Submission accepted only through the shared Google Drive link
Upload window is limited to 2 hours after closing ceremony
Late submissions are eliminated

details.team_structure: Solo or group participation (maximum 2 members).
additional_information: Maximum entries: 30 (solo + group combined)
Participants must attach payment screenshot and transaction ID in registration
For queries contact Arya Sutar at 7666887603

events.description: Theme-based 3D modeling challenge to build realistic scenes using creativity, composition, and rendering skills.

rounds.name: Theme-Based 3D Modeling
rounds.subtitle: One-day challenge
rounds.description: Participants create a visually realistic 3D scene from a theme announced at the event start.
steps: Theme announced at the beginning of challenge
Model and arrange assets creatively within time limit
Submit project file and rendered image before deadline

details.primary_overview: Clash of 3D Creators challenges participants to build immersive 3D scenes under a given theme while showcasing visual composition and realism.
details.what_is: A one-day 3D modeling competition where participants can use any software and online assets, but originality and presentation quality drive scoring.
who_can_participate: Open to all students from any department or year

rules_and_regulations.title: Participation Rules
points: Participants must carry valid college ID card
Participants must bring their own laptop, software setup, and mouse
Respectful behavior is mandatory

rules_and_regulations.title: Competition Rules
points: Theme will be disclosed only at challenge start
Submissions must align with the provided theme
Use of online assets is allowed, but final scene composition must be original
Submit both source file and rendered output (PNG/JPEG)

rules_and_regulations.title: Judging Criteria
points: Theme relevance
Creativity and arrangement
Realism and detailing
Overall visual impact

details.team_structure: Individual or team participation up to 2 members.
additional_information: Participation includes certificates and refreshments
For queries contact Rohit Harne at 8459206177

events.description: Project presentation and demonstration competition for E&TC domain projects.

participation.eligibility: Any project from Electronics and Telecommunication field

rounds.name: Project Presentation and Demonstration
rounds.subtitle: Single evaluation round
rounds.description: Teams present and demonstrate their E&TC project solution to the judges.

details.primary_overview: Project Competition is a technical showcase where participants present and demonstrate Electronics and Telecommunication projects before a judging panel.
details.what_is: The event evaluates project quality, implementation clarity, and presentation effectiveness for E&TC-focused ideas.
who_can_participate: Participants with projects from Electronics and Telecommunication field
Registered participants only
Maximum 5 participants per team

rules_and_regulations.title: Project Competition Rules
points: Maximum 5 participants per team are allowed
Only registered participants will be allowed
Participants must bring their own laptop/systems for presentation and demonstration
Participants should carry required hardware/software for their project
Every participant will receive a certificate
Judges' decision will be final

details.team_structure: Individual and team participation allowed, with up to 5 members per team.
additional_information: Registration link: https://forms.gle/w5Ku7kv9fKdtxBSb6

events.description: Robotics soccer competition with goal-based qualification rounds.

participation.eligibility: First year to final year students

rounds.name: Match Qualifier
rounds.subtitle: Goal-based qualification
rounds.description: Teams compete in two halves of a 3-minute match and highest goal count qualifies for next round.

rounds.name: Knockout Rounds
rounds.subtitle: Final elimination matches
rounds.description: Qualified teams compete in elimination matches to determine final standings.

details.primary_overview: Robo Soccer is a team robotics challenge where bots compete in soccer-style matches and teams qualify based on goals scored.
details.what_is: The event tests robot control, strategy, and mechanical compliance under timed match conditions.
who_can_participate: Open for first year to final year students
Team participation only
Team size: minimum 2 and maximum 4 members
event_timeline: Registration Fee: INR 200 (individual reference), team fee range INR 400 to INR 600

rules_and_regulations.title: Robo Soccer Rules
points: Each team must have minimum 2 and maximum 4 members
Each robot is allowed only one attachment for hitting or controlling the ball
Team scoring maximum goals within two halves of a 3-minute match qualifies for next round
Judges' decisions regarding results, robot specifications, and event issues are final and binding
Robot size limit: 30 x 30 x 30 and weight between 3 kg and 5 kg

details.team_structure: Team participation only with 2 to 4 members.
additional_information: Team fee can vary between INR 400 and INR 600 based on team format
Registration link: https://forms.gle/7GoNhnzDiRx5kU1i6

events.description: Circuit design and simulation competition under time constraints.

participation.eligibility: First year to final year students

rounds.name: Circuit Design and Simulation
rounds.subtitle: Timed technical challenge
rounds.description: Participants design and simulate circuits within the allotted time and are evaluated on correctness and efficiency.

details.primary_overview: Circuit Nexus is a circuit design and simulation event that evaluates technical accuracy, logic, and speed.
details.what_is: Participants solve a design problem by creating and simulating circuits within a fixed time window.
who_can_participate: Open for first year to final year students
Individual and team participation allowed
Maximum 2 participants per team

rules_and_regulations.title: Circuit Nexus Rules
points: Maximum 2 participants per team are allowed
Only registered participants will be allowed to participate
Participants must carry their own laptops if required by organizers
The circuit must be designed and simulated within the given time limit
Use of pre-designed circuits, external files, or internet resources is strictly prohibited
Any malfunction, software crash, or system issue must be immediately reported to coordinators
Evaluation is based on circuit accuracy, simulation results, innovation/logic, and time efficiency
Judges' decision is final and binding

details.team_structure: Individual and team participation allowed, with a maximum of 2 members per team.
additional_information: Registration link: https://forms.gle/QxBGefaSThqyVtGk8

departments.department_name: Computer Science and Engineering (Artificial Intelligence - Machine Learning)

events.description: Fast-paced AI-assisted coding competition focused on rapid prototyping and creative problem-solving.

participation.eligibility: Students from all departments (Diploma and UG)

rounds.name: Development Phase
rounds.subtitle: Round 1
rounds.description: Participants build a working prototype using AI tools and submit a public GitHub repository before deadline.
steps: Build functional prototype using AI tools
Mandatory GitHub submission with README and source code

rounds.name: Evaluation and Presentation
rounds.subtitle: Round 2
rounds.description: Shortlisted teams present and demo their solution with AI usage and real-world relevance.

details.primary_overview: VIBETHON is an AI-powered coding event that simulates a real-world hackathon environment through rapid prototyping and practical implementation.
details.what_is: The event emphasizes creative problem-solving, effective use of AI tools, and original implementation under strict time limits.
who_can_participate: Students from Diploma and Undergraduate programs
Students from all departments are eligible
Special encouragement for first year participation
event_timeline: Time: 10:00 AM to 04:00 PM (including evaluation and result)
Registration Fee: INR 120 (General), INR 100 for first-year participants valid for first 36 entries

rules_and_regulations.title: VIBETHON Rules and Guidelines
points: Use of AI tools such as ChatGPT and Copilot is allowed
GitHub submission is compulsory
Repository must be public and include README plus source code
Pre-built or previously developed projects are strictly prohibited
Idea and logic must be original
Internet usage is allowed during event
Teams must complete development within given timeframe
Late submissions will not be considered
Any plagiarism or malpractice leads to disqualification

rules_and_regulations.title: Judging Criteria
points: Innovation and Idea: 20%
Implementation and Functionality: 30%
Effective Use of AI Tools: 20%
Presentation and Communication: 15%
Real-world Applicability: 15%

details.team_structure: Participation format can be individual or team as per event flow.
additional_information: Refreshment break: 1:00 PM to 1:30 PM
Top 8 to 10 teams are shortlisted for final presentation
Venue details inside DYPCET campus will be shared before event

events.description: Investigation-style case-solving challenge with quiz, observation, and final mystery rounds.

participation.eligibility: Open to all students (Diploma and UG)

rounds.name: Quiz Round
rounds.subtitle: Round 1
rounds.description: Offline quiz to test logical reasoning and general awareness.

rounds.name: Observation Round
rounds.subtitle: Round 2
rounds.description: Teams analyze images, clues, and scenarios for two hours.

rounds.name: Final Case-Solving Round
rounds.subtitle: Round 3
rounds.description: Teams solve a complete mystery case using provided clues.

details.primary_overview: Killer Mystery is a team-based problem-solving event that tests observation, logic, and decision-making through progressive investigative rounds.
details.what_is: Participants move through quiz and clue-analysis stages before solving a final complete mystery case.
who_can_participate: Open to all undergraduate and diploma students
Individual or team participation allowed
Maximum 2 members per team
event_timeline: Registration Fee: INR 100 per participant or team

rules_and_regulations.title: Event Structure and Workflow
points: Workflow: Registration -> Round 1 -> Refreshment Break -> Round 2 -> Round 3 -> Result Declaration
Screening is based on performance in each round
Accuracy and speed are key evaluation factors
Top teams are shortlisted after each stage

rules_and_regulations.title: General Rules
points: Participants must follow coordinators' instructions
Time limits must be strictly maintained
Unfair means are strictly prohibited
Teams must rely on their own observation and logic
Judges' decision is final and binding
Misconduct or malpractice leads to disqualification

details.team_structure: Individual participation or team of up to 2 members.
additional_information: Venue details inside DYPCET campus will be shared before event

events.description: Electrifying esports showdown featuring Free Fire and PES / eFootball formats.

participation.eligibility: Open to all college students (Diploma and UG)

rounds.name: Free Fire Competitive Round
rounds.subtitle: Points-based stage
rounds.description: Teams compete in Free Fire rounds with placement points and kill points.
steps: Custom lobby instructions shared on WhatsApp group

rounds.name: PES / eFootball Knockout
rounds.subtitle: Elimination stage
rounds.description: PES / eFootball participants compete in knockout matches.

details.primary_overview: Dare to Battle 3.0 is an esports event testing reflexes, strategy, and teamwork through Free Fire and PES / eFootball matches.
details.what_is: Participants battle through ranked and knockout formats for cash prizes, MVP recognition, and event glory.
who_can_participate: Students from Diploma and Undergraduate programs
Open to all college students
event_timeline: Time: 10:00 AM to 04:00 PM (qualifiers and finals)
Registration Fee: INR 400 per squad (Free Fire), INR 100 per head (PES)

rules_and_regulations.title: Game Instructions
points: Game will be played in offline mode
Any cheating or hacking leads to immediate disqualification
Custom match instructions are communicated through designated WhatsApp group
Coordinators' decisions on rules and disputes are final
No refunds will be provided
Participants can join only one game: Free Fire or PES

rules_and_regulations.title: Free Fire Points Table
points: Each kill = 1 point

details.team_structure: Format includes squad-based and individual participation depending on game mode.
additional_information: Venue: Classroom 306 and 307 at DYPCET
PES is played in knockout format

events.description: Tower-building challenge using up to 150 popsicle sticks, judged by structural efficiency ratio.
events.venue: Geo-Technical Engineering Lab, Civil Engineering Department

rounds.name: Design and Build
rounds.subtitle: Single structural challenge
rounds.description: Teams construct a popsicle-stick tower meeting dimensional constraints, followed by centralized load testing.
steps: Construct tower with maximum 150 popsicle sticks
Maintain cross-section limit of 150 mm x 150 mm
Achieve minimum tower height of 600 mm
Central vertical load test and efficiency ratio evaluation

details.primary_overview: TowerQuest challenges participants to build a lightweight but strong tower and maximize load-carrying efficiency.
details.what_is: Participants design and construct a tower using popsicle sticks and adhesive, then compete on load-to-weight performance.
who_can_participate: Open to Degree and Diploma students
Individual or team participation allowed
Maximum 2 participants per team

rules_and_regulations.title: TowerQuest Rules
points: Maximum 2 participants per team
Only ice-cream sticks and Fevicol are allowed
Total truss cross section should be limited to 150 mm x 150 mm
Load will be applied at the center of the structure
Strength-to-weight or load-to-weight ratio will decide the winner
Shape profile is final; internal stick arrangement can vary
Judges' decision is final

details.team_structure: Individual participation or team of up to 2 members.
additional_information: Tower must reach minimum height of 600 mm
Event is judged by efficiency ratio (load held vs structural weight)

events.description: Concrete mix design event targeting 40 N/mm2 compressive strength after 7-day curing.
events.venue: Material Testing Lab, Civil Engineering Department

rounds.name: Mix Design and Casting
rounds.subtitle: Single practical round
rounds.description: Participants prepare one concrete cube and target compressive strength close to 40 N/mm2 at 7 days.
steps: Prepare one concrete cube of size 150 mm x 150 mm x 150 mm
Evaluate compressive strength and ratio criteria

details.primary_overview: Cube Crush Challenge tests practical concrete mix design skills by targeting a specified compressive strength within a fixed curing timeline.
details.what_is: Participants cast a single concrete cube and are evaluated based on strength accuracy and efficiency.
who_can_participate: Open to Degree and Diploma students
Individual or team participation allowed
Maximum 2 participants per team

rules_and_regulations.title: Cube Crush Rules
points: Prepare only one concrete cube targeting 40 N/mm2 at 7-day curing
Mix design report must be signed by HOD of respective institute
Cube size must be 150 mm x 150 mm x 150 mm
Use of chemical admixture is allowed
Use of fiber, mesh, or reinforcement is not allowed
Cube strength nearest to plus or minus 40 N/mm2 will be preferred
Strength-to-weight ratio will be considered for final result

details.team_structure: Individual participation or team of up to 2 members.
additional_information: Target compressive strength is checked after 7-day curing period

events.description: CAD competition to test digital design precision, speed, and presentation quality.

rounds.name: Scrutiny Round
rounds.subtitle: Round 1 - MCQ
rounds.description: MCQ screening round with negative marking.
steps: Negative marking of 1 mark per wrong answer

rounds.name: AutoCAD Design Round
rounds.subtitle: Round 2
rounds.description: Participants prepare a plan on AutoCAD using college-provided computers.
steps: Plan must include all required details

rounds.name: Tie-Breaker Round
rounds.subtitle: Round 3 (if required)
rounds.description: Additional half-hour round conducted only in case of tie.

details.primary_overview: The Designer is a CAD-focused competition that evaluates participants on technical drawing accuracy, speed, and presentation quality.
details.what_is: Participants solve a design problem by converting technical concepts into detailed digital CAD representations.
who_can_participate: Open to Degree and Diploma students
Individual participation only

rules_and_regulations.title: Round Rules
points: Round 1 is MCQ-based scrutiny round
Round 2 is AutoCAD plan design round
Round 3 is tie-breaker only if needed
College computers will be provided
Personal laptops are not allowed

rules_and_regulations.title: General Rules and Evaluation
points: Cheating leads to immediate elimination
Mobile phones and electronic devices are not allowed
Evaluation includes timely completion, orientation, presentation, and technical factors such as scale, text, accuracy, dimensions, hatching, and file size
Judges' decision is final

details.team_structure: Only single-participant entries are allowed.
additional_information: AutoCAD round file should satisfy minimum size requirement as per event instructions

events.description: Three-round civil engineering challenge testing materials knowledge, structural diagnosis, and rapid analysis under pressure.

rounds.name: Blindfold Round
rounds.subtitle: Round 1
rounds.description: Participants identify construction materials by touch within a strict time limit.
steps: Blindfold remains on throughout the round

rounds.name: Identify Me
rounds.subtitle: Round 2
rounds.description: Teams analyze structural problem images and explain causes with suitable solutions.
steps: Qualification based on completeness and correctness

rounds.name: Structure Beauty
rounds.subtitle: Round 3
rounds.description: Rapid-fire final based on structural images after short preparation.

details.primary_overview: Civil Clash is a multi-round event that combines tactile material recognition, structural fault analysis, and quick-response engineering thinking.
details.what_is: Participants compete solo or in duo teams through three progressive rounds to demonstrate technical insight and problem-solving speed.
who_can_participate: Open to Degree and Diploma students
Single or duo entry allowed
Qualification is based on Round 1 and Round 2 performance

rules_and_regulations.title: Round Rules
points: Round 1 (Blindfold): identify given material by touch with 60 seconds per guess
Round 2 (Identify Me): identify structural problem, explain causes, and suggest solutions
Round 3 (Structure Beauty): 10 minutes preparation followed by rapid-fire questions

rules_and_regulations.title: General Rules
points: Single or duo participation is allowed
Winner and runner-up are decided from Round 3
Cheating leads to immediate elimination
Mobile phones and other electronic devices are not allowed
Judges' decision is final

details.team_structure: Individual participation or team of up to 2 members.
additional_information: Civil Clash includes three rounds with qualification before final stage

events.description: Cross-disciplinary team quiz with MCQ, visual, and rapid-fire rounds focused on logic, awareness, and problem-solving.
events.venue: Classroom 401, School of Architecture (4th Floor), DYPSEM, Kasba Bawada, Kolhapur

participation.eligibility: Open to all students from any department and year

rounds.name: General Knowledge Round
rounds.subtitle: Round 1 - MCQ
rounds.description: Multiple-choice round scored by answer accuracy.

rounds.name: Visual and Scenario Round
rounds.subtitle: Round 2
rounds.description: Teams identify displayed visuals and answer scenario-based clues with limited response time.

rounds.name: Rapid Fire Final
rounds.subtitle: Round 3 - Head to Head
rounds.description: Final one-on-one rapid-fire battle between shortlisted top teams.

details.primary_overview: MindMesh Quiz is a cross-disciplinary challenge that combines knowledge, speed, teamwork, and real-world thinking in a dynamic competition format.
details.what_is: The event includes multiple rounds such as MCQ, visual/scenario analysis, and rapid-fire finals to test interdisciplinary awareness and collaborative problem-solving.
who_can_participate: Open to all students irrespective of department
Individual participation allowed
Teams of 2 members allowed

rules_and_regulations.title: Registration and Attendance
points: Each team must register through the Google form
Registration closes on 14 Apr 2026 at 12:00 noon
No on-the-spot entries are allowed
Participants must report 30 minutes before event start
Late participants will not be allowed

rules_and_regulations.title: Conduct and Fair Play
points: Each group can have only two members
Participants must maintain respect toward hosts and other competitors
No disputes regarding scores or decisions will be entertained
Any cheating, disruptive behavior, or unauthorized material use leads to disqualification

details.team_structure: Individual participation or team of 2 members.
additional_information: Venue block and room details may be shared again before event
Prize winners receive cash and certificates

events.event_name: Modular partition unit from Trash to Treasure
events.description: Sustainable design challenge to build modular partition systems from recycled or waste materials.
events.venue: School of Architecture (4th Floor), DYPSEM, Kasba Bawada, Kolhapur

rounds.name: Design and Build Module
rounds.subtitle: Main competition round
rounds.description: Participants design and construct a modular partition unit using primarily waste and recycled materials.
steps: Use at least 70 percent waste or recycled materials
Create lightweight and structurally stable repeatable module
Allow light, ventilation, and visual permeability

details.primary_overview: This competition transforms waste into functional architecture by creating modular partition units suitable for indoor or outdoor use.
details.what_is: Participants design and fabricate a repeatable partition system balancing sustainability, aesthetics, and structural stability.
who_can_participate: Architecture and design students
Individual participation allowed
Teams of 2 to 3 students allowed
Each team can submit only one entry

rules_and_regulations.title: Design Requirements
points: Use minimum 70 percent waste or recycled materials
Module should function as screen, divider, facade element, or shading device
Design must be modular and repeatable horizontally or vertically
Partition should be lightweight yet structurally stable
Design should allow light, ventilation, and visual permeability

rules_and_regulations.title: Participation and Conduct
points: Participation can be individual or teams of 2 to 3 students
Each team can submit one entry only
Model must be constructed by participants
Market-ready decorative panels are not allowed
Plagiarized content, designs, or ideas lead to disqualification
Safety precautions must be followed during fabrication
Each group gets 3 hours to complete design
Teams should report 30 minutes before start
No late or on-the-spot entries are allowed

rules_and_regulations.title: Evaluation Criteria
points: Innovation in waste material: 25%
Structural stability: 20%
Aesthetic quality: 20%
Modular repeatability: 15%
Sustainability: 10%
Ease of fabrication: 10%

details.team_structure: Individual entries or teams of 2 to 3 students.
additional_information: Recommended module frame size is 2 feet x 4 feet
Optional constraints may include max 3 waste materials and budget cap of INR 1000
Selected designs may be displayed on campus

events.description: A simulation of real campus recruitment procedures.
events.venue: DY Patil College of Engineering & Technology, Kolhapur

participation.eligibility: Open to all departments of engineering students across India

rounds.name: General Aptitude Test (GAT)
rounds.subtitle: Round 1
rounds.description: Test includes questions on quantitative aptitude, logical reasoning, and verbal ability.
steps: Shortlisting purely based on assessment scores

rounds.name: Group Discussion (GD)
rounds.subtitle: Round 2
rounds.description: Topics based on current affairs and general issues.
steps: Evaluated on communication, confidence, leadership, and analytical skills

rounds.name: Personal Interview (PI)
rounds.subtitle: Round 3
rounds.description: Interviews by a professional HR panel.
steps: Assessment based on technical knowledge, HR skills, personality, and overall presentation

details.primary_overview: The Mock Placement Drive consists of three structured rounds designed to simulate real campus recruitment procedures.
details.what_is: A competition that helps students experience real-life recruitment processes.
who_can_participate: Open to all departments of engineering students across India

rules_and_regulations.title: Guidelines
points: All participants must appear along with formal outfits and with their Resume + Folder file of all supporting documents for Personal Interviews.
All Participants must have to appear for Round 1.
Based on performance, shortlisted candidates will progress to further rounds.

details.team_structure: Individual/Solo
additional_information: Faculty Coordinators - Lt.(Dr.) Rahul D. Mahajan, Mrs. Poonam R. Mandale, Mr. Sumit Potdar
Non teaching Faculty - Mr. Digvijay Bhogavkar

events.description: Design, develop, and present a novel product that addresses real-world needs.

rounds.name: Presentation and Evaluation
rounds.subtitle: Round 1
rounds.description: Evaluation based on Usefulness to the society, Enovation, cost effective, feasibility and understanding.

details.primary_overview: This competition challenges participants to design, develop, and present a novel product that addresses real-world needs. Teams will work through stages of ideation, prototyping, and validation.
details.what_is: A product development competition where innovation meets impact.
who_can_participate: Students from Diploma and Undergraduate (UG) programs are eligible to participate.

rules_and_regulations.title: Rules & Regulations
points: There shall be minimum 3 and maximum 4 members in a team.
Students should bring the sample of the product and has to showcase along with its information.
Students can prepare PPT/Poster presentation to give the information about the product.
Only original Product Development ideas allowed (no copied ideas).
Time limits for presentation (5–10 min).

details.team_structure: Minimum 3 and maximum 4 members in a team.
additional_information: Block/Room details will be shared before the event

events.description: Showcasing your mini and mega projects in the field of Project areas Chemical, biotechnology, pharmaceutical, nanotechnology, environmental.

rounds.name: Presentation and Evaluation
rounds.subtitle: Round 1
rounds.description: Evaluation will be done on the basis of Usefulness to society, Innovation, Cost effective, feasibility and understanding.

details.primary_overview: Showcasing your mini and mega projects in the field of Project areas Chemical, biotechnology, pharmaceutical, nanotechnology, environmental and any project related to betterment of society.
details.what_is: A competition to showcase innovative projects.
who_can_participate: Students from Diploma and Undergraduate (UG) programs are eligible to participate.

rules_and_regulations.title: Rules & Regulations
points: There shall be minimum 3 and maximum 5 members in a team.
Project should be in following areas: Chemical, biotechnology, pharmaceutical, nanotechnology, environmental and any project related to betterment of society.
Students can present their project in the form of prototype, ppt, design or any other form to give the information about the project.
Project will be evaluated on the basis of Usefulness to the society, Innovation, Cost effective, feasibility and understanding.

details.team_structure: Minimum 3 and maximum 5 members in a team.
additional_information: Block/Room details will be shared before the event

events.description: A two-stage quiz and live auction event replicating the Indian Premier League auction strategy environment.

rounds.name: IPL Quiz (Qualifier)
rounds.subtitle: Round 1
rounds.description: Teams answer IPL-focused quiz questions on players, teams, stats, and records.
steps: Quiz on IPL teams, players, stats, and records

rounds.name: IPL Quiz (Elimination)
rounds.subtitle: Round 2
rounds.description: Elimination round with advanced IPL quiz questions.

rounds.name: Live IPL Auction
rounds.subtitle: Round 3
rounds.description: Qualified teams are assigned an IPL franchise and build squads through live bidding with a virtual purse.
steps: Auction with INR 100 crore virtual purse
Squad build as per role and foreign-player rules
Winner decided by balance, ratings, and purse strategy

details.primary_overview: IPL Auction recreates the real auction atmosphere where student teams use cricket knowledge and strategy to build Dream11-style squads.
details.what_is: The event blends quiz-based qualification with a live bidding simulation to test decision-making, budget management, and team composition strategy.
who_can_participate: Open to all students from any department or year
Team participation only
Each team must have 3 to 4 members
event_timeline: Team fee: INR 300 to INR 400 based on team size

rules_and_regulations.title: Team and Qualification Rules
points: Total teams: 40
Each team must have 3 to 4 members
Top 10 teams qualify for Mega Auction Round
Qualified teams are assigned one IPL franchise randomly

rules_and_regulations.title: Auction and Purse Rules
points: Each team gets an INR 100 crore virtual purse
Bid increment: INR 50 lakh till INR 10 crore
Bid increment: INR 1 crore after INR 10 crore
Teams must remain within purse budget

rules_and_regulations.title: Squad Composition Rules
points: Minimum 5 batsmen
Minimum 1 wicketkeeper
Minimum 2 all-rounders
Minimum 3 bowlers
Maximum 4 foreign players
Minimum 2 female players in team

rules_and_regulations.title: General Notes
points: Rules may vary on the event day
Committee decision is final

details.team_structure: Team participation with minimum 3 and maximum 4 members.
additional_information: Winner criteria: team balance, player rating, purse utilization, and rule compliance
Live animated auction setup with team management sheets
For queries contact Abhijeet Adamuthe at 7058987796

events.description: Participants present their research papers on technical themes.

rounds.name: Round - 1
rounds.subtitle: Paper Presentation
rounds.description: Time Limit: 6 Minutes Presentation + 2 Minutes Q&A
steps: Follow IEEE format or provided template
Target technical and research-based topic from list

details.primary_overview: Technical Paper Presentation provides a platform for students to present original research on advanced engineering themes including IoT, Smart Cities, AI, and Cybersecurity.
details.what_is: A competition testing research depth, technical accuracy, and presentation skills on specific forward-looking engineering topics.
who_can_participate: Open for all department
A maximum of 4 participants per team is allowed.

rules_and_regulations.title: Submission & Format Rules
points: The paper must follow the IEEE format or shared format.
Topic should be technical and research-based from the list.
Submissions must be original and plagiarism-free.
Deadline for paper submission: 14 April 2026
Submit paper to dypcettechnotsav25@gmail.com with subject TeamLeaderName_CollegeName_TopicNumber.

rules_and_regulations.title: Presentation Guidelines
points: PPT must be prepared using the official template.
Use of PowerPoint (.ppt/.pptx) or PDF format is allowed.
6 minutes for presentation, 2 minutes for Q&A.

rules_and_regulations.title: Judging Criteria
points: Research Depth & Originality.
Technical Content & Accuracy.
Presentation & Communication Skills.
Handling of Questions (Q&A).

details.team_structure: A maximum of 4 participants per team is allowed.
additional_information: The entire event will be conducted in offline mode.
Students must bring a valid college ID card for verification.
The judges’ decision will be final. No objections or disputes will be entertained.
Acceptance will be declared by 14 April 2025(via mail).

events.description: Design and develop interactive games using AI-assisted development within deadline.

rounds.name: Rapid Quiz (Elimination Round)
rounds.subtitle: Round 1
rounds.description: AI and technology-based MCQs, game development basics, logical reasoning.
steps: Top teams qualify for the development round

rounds.name: Game Development
rounds.subtitle: Round 2
rounds.description: Teams must develop a playable game based on the given problem statement.
steps: Focus on gameplay mechanics, UX, creativity

rounds.name: Testing & Judging
rounds.subtitle: Round 3
rounds.description: Judges test the games and teams explain their approach.
steps: Explain design decisions and AI usage approach

details.primary_overview: The GameCraft – AI Game Development Challenge provides a platform for students to design and develop creative, interactive, and user-friendly games using controlled AI tools.
details.what_is: The competition blends game development, AI integration, and UX design, allowing participants to showcase their technical and creative abilities.
who_can_participate: Open to all Students of any Department or Year
Team participation only

rules_and_regulations.title: Development Rules
points: The competition duration is strictly 5 hours.
Problem statement/theme given at the start.
Use of any programming language or framework (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, etc.) is allowed.

rules_and_regulations.title: AI Usage Rules
points: Only AI tools provided by the organizers are allowed.
Use of external AI tools is strictly prohibited.
Participants must demonstrate understanding of AI-generated outputs.

details.team_structure: Minimum 2 and Maximum 3 members per team.
additional_information: Participants must carry their own laptops and necessary accessories

events.description: A fast-paced data analytics competition where participants design and develop interactive dashboards.

rounds.name: Dashboard Development & Presentation
rounds.subtitle: Round 1
rounds.description: Develop and present an interactive dashboard based on a given dataset.
steps: Presentation (5 Min), Demonstration (3 Min), Q&A (5 Min)

details.primary_overview: BI DashBattle is a fast-paced data analytics competition where participants design interactive dashboards based on a given dataset or business problem statement.
details.what_is: A competition to showcase analytical thinking, visualization skills, and problem-solving abilities using tools like Power BI or Tableau.
who_can_participate: Open to all Students of any Department or Year

rules_and_regulations.title: Development Rules
points: Time Limit: 2 Hours.
Teams must report to the venue at least 30 minutes before the start.
Use of personal laptops, mobile phones, internet, or pen drives is strictly prohibited.
Participants must use only the systems provided in the computer lab.

details.team_structure: Individuals or teams (maximum 2 member per team).
additional_information: For any queries, reach out to Namrata Belvekar.

events.description: Competitive Battlegrounds Mobile India tournament in battle royale mode.

rounds.name: Battle Royale Match
rounds.subtitle: Round 1
rounds.description: Teams fight in battle royale matches.
steps: Scoring based on kills and final ranking

details.primary_overview: The E-sports is a 4-hour fast-paced competition where participants compete in Battlegrounds Mobile India tournaments for prize money.
details.what_is: A squad-based mobile gaming tournament.
who_can_participate: All students from any Department

rules_and_regulations.title: Rules
points: Each team has 4 players (squad).
Matches are played in battle royale mode.
Points based on Kills + Final position (ranking).
Fair Play: No hacking or cheating, no external tools.
Communication: Only team members allowed.
No abusive language or toxic behavior.

details.team_structure: 4 players per team (Squad)

events.description: A thrilling contest where students build and control robots to race through an obstacle track.

participation.eligibility: Students from Diploma and Undergraduate (UG) programs

rounds.name: Robo Race
rounds.subtitle: Track completion
rounds.description: The robot with the lowest total time (including penalties) will be declared the winner.
steps: Avoid penalties for skipping obstacles or touching the robot

details.primary_overview: Robo Race is a thrilling contest where students build and control robots to race through a track, testing speed, precision, and obstacle navigation while encouraging innovation and teamwork.
details.what_is: A robotic racing competition against time on an obstacle-filled track.
who_can_participate: Students from Diploma and Undergraduate (UG) programs
event_timeline: Spot Entry Fee: INR 250 per person

rules_and_regulations.title: Timer Start & End
points: The timer starts when the robot begins moving from the starting line.
The timer stops only when the robot crosses the finish line.
If the robot encounters issues, only one opportunity will be given to fix defects.

rules_and_regulations.title: Track Rules & Penalties
points: The robot must stay on the designated track.
If the robot goes off-track, it must return to the last checkpoint crossed.
Skipping any obstacle: 30-second penalty per obstacle.
Touching the robot by hand: 10-second penalty per instance.

rules_and_regulations.title: Robot Specifications
points: Control: Manually operated (wired or wireless).
Size Limit: Maximum 250mm × 250mm × 250mm.
Weight Limit: Maximum 3 kg.
Use of Lego parts or ready-made kits is not allowed.
Max 12V battery permitted. Teams must carry an extra battery.

details.team_structure: Maximum of 2 members per team.
additional_information: Robots provided by the committee will be allotted on a first-come, first-serve basis for spot entries.

events.description: Showcase your creativity, speed, and technical skills in computer-aided design.

participation.eligibility: Students from Diploma and Undergraduate (UG) programs

rounds.name: Quiz Round
rounds.subtitle: Round 1 (Elimination)
rounds.description: An online quiz testing basic knowledge about CAD software, design standards and general engineering concepts.
steps: Answer multiple-choice questions within a limited time.

rounds.name: 2D Drafting on AutoCAD
rounds.subtitle: Round 2 (Elimination)
rounds.description: Create a 2D drawing of a given drawing using AutoCAD.

rounds.name: 3D Modelling Round
rounds.subtitle: Round 3
rounds.description: Create a 3D model of a mechanical part using CAD software.

details.primary_overview: CAD Clash tests participants' knowledge and hands-on abilities in design software through multiple engaging rounds including quizzing, 2D drafting, and 3D modeling.
details.what_is: A multi-round computer-aided design competition.
who_can_participate: Students from Diploma and Undergraduate (UG) programs
event_timeline: Venue: Classroom 139, CAD Lab 1 & 2, CIII Lab

rules_and_regulations.title: General Rules
points: Only individual participation is allowed.
Participants must bring smartphones for Round 1.
Round 2 will be conducted strictly on AutoCAD on provided systems.
UG NX 9.0 will be provided for the final round. Competitors can bring their laptop with other CAD software pre-installed.

rules_and_regulations.title: Judging Criteria
points: Final round judged on minimum number of steps/features used.
Minimum time taken to complete the design.

details.team_structure: Individual Participation
additional_information: Refreshments will be provided.
Every participant will receive a Participation Certificate.

events.description: A rapid-fire technical debate competition to test your communication and engineering knowledge.

rounds.name: Quiz Round
rounds.subtitle: Round 1 (Elimination)
rounds.description: A Quiz on general Engineering e.g. Mechanical, Automobile etc.

rounds.name: Tech Debate
rounds.subtitle: Round 2
rounds.description: Debate on a technical topic randomly picked from 'CHITS BOWL' on the spot.

rounds.name: Final Round
rounds.subtitle: Round 3
rounds.description: Shortlisted teams will pick topics again for a final debate.

details.primary_overview: Tech Debate challenges participants to quickly synthesize their technical knowledge and debate impromptu on engineering topics.
details.what_is: A competition consisting of a technical quiz followed by multiple rounds of spot debating on engineering concepts.
who_can_participate: Open for All

rules_and_regulations.title: Debate Rules
points: Language of conduct – English
Topics will be given on the spot from a 'CHITS BOWL'.
Every participant should speak during the debate.
Abusive language and misconduct are strictly prohibited.
No smartphones allowed during final round preparation.

details.team_structure: Maximum allowed entries per group is 2.
